observations.summary: The e2e-tests step found AC-5 (scroll-to-bottom button) non-functional — ChatMessageList tracks scroll position but never updates ConversationPane's showScrollToBottom state — documented it as deferred, but no downstream step fixed it and the story was marked done with a known AC gap.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for e2e-tests: 'Deferred (AC-5): The scroll-to-bottom button has an implementation gap — ChatMessageList tracks scroll position but never updates ConversationPane's showScrollToBottom state, so the button never appears. Documented in the summary.' No subsequent step_end mentions resolving it; review-code marked story status 'done'.

observations.summary: The review-code step reviewed only chunk 1 of 4 (agent-be backend core); the web frontend production code (chunk 3, containing the AC-5 gap) was never reviewed, and no follow-up mechanism exists in the playbook to pick up remaining chunks.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-code: 'Chunk 1 of 4 — agent-be backend core... Remaining chunks for follow-up: (2) agent-be tests, (3) web frontend production, (4) web frontend tests + e2e + config.' Playbook prompt for review-code says 'take the first chunk automatically and note remaining groups for a follow-up run' but no such follow-up step exists.

observations.summary: 8 test.skip() calls in Playwright E2E tests from stories 1.2, 1.3, and 2.3 persist in the codebase; the unit-tests step correctly defers them as out of scope (DP-5) on every story, but no step ever resolves them, so they will recur as noise on every future run.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for unit-tests: 'Skipped tests: Zero in Story 3.3 scope. 8 test.skip() calls found in Playwright E2E tests from earlier stories (1.2, 1.3, 2.3) — deferred per DP-5 (out of scope).'

observations.summary: 6 hard waits (setTimeout(resolve, 50)) in fire-and-forget test files — the same non-deterministic pattern flagged in Story 3.1 — were caught by review-tests as a P1 weakness but left unfixed; implement-story keeps producing this pattern.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-tests: 'P1: 6 hard waits (setTimeout(resolve, 50)) in agent.service.spec.ts and conversations.service.spec.ts for fire-and-forget testing — same non-deterministic pattern flagged in Story 3.1'. Story 3.1 review-tests also flagged this pattern (per trends context).
